etymology.description: semantic 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") + phonetic 夭(夭) (yāo, "young") -> laugh
etymology.interpretation: The character 笑(xiào, "to laugh; to smile") combines 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") as the semantic component, suggesting thinness or stalk-like shapes associated with laughter lines on a face, with 夭(夭) (yāo, "young") as the phonetic component, conveying the sound. The combination captures the light and joyous action of laughing or smiling, akin to the lively and spirited traits associated with youth.

examples.chinese: 看到他的好消息(看到他的好消息)，我忍不住笑了(我忍不住笑了)。
examples.pinyin: Kàndào tā de hǎo xiāoxī，wǒ rěnbùzhù xiào le.
examples.english: Seeing his good news, I couldn't help but laugh.

examples.chinese: 孩子们在操场上玩耍(孩子們在操場上玩耍)，快乐地笑着(快樂地笑著)。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen zài cāochǎng shàng wánshuǎ，kuàilè de xiào zhe.
examples.english: The children are playing on the playground, smiling happily.

examples.chinese: 她的笑容(她的笑容)总是让人感到温暖(總是讓人感到溫暖)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xiàoróng zǒng shì ràng rén gǎndào wēnnuǎn.
examples.english: Her smile always makes people feel warm.
